title: Niphobolus glaber
taxonomy: Niphobolus glaber is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. It belongs to the order Polypodiales, a group of vascular plants that are characterized by their fern-like leaves. The genus Niphobolus is a small group of ferns, and Niphobolus glaber is one of the species within this genus. The species is further classified into the subgenus Niphobolus. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Niphobolus glaber within the plant kingdom.

The plant is believed to possess sedative properties, which help in calming the nervous system. intro: Niphobolus glaber, also known as glabrous maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Niphobolus glaber include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Niphobolus glaber is often made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
